steps.title: Identify the relative clause position
steps.content: The blank will be filled with a noun (“Trail”) followed by a relative clause (“which is 4.36 miles long”). This clause is introduced by “which” and appears between the subject noun phrase and the main verb “have become.”

steps.title: Apply the non-restrictive clause punctuation rule
steps.content: Relative clauses introduced by “which” are non-restrictive and must be set off with commas on both sides when they appear in the middle of a sentence. Choice D correctly places both required commas: “Trail, which is 4.36 miles long,” — one before the clause and one after. The other choices violate this rule by omitting one or both of these commas or substituting incorrect punctuation. That matches choice D — D is correct.
